metadata.title: Vox-Style Explainers, Explained
metadata.notes: THE META FILM — a Vox explainer teaching how to make a Vox explainer; the worked example is itself. Fully recursive: DOCUMENT beats show THIS reel's files; B15 slate and B21 burn-in are frames harvested from this reel's own pass-1/review cuts (harvest order in SHOTLIST). Playlist pilot for 'Unreal Reels (Vox Style)'. REVISED 2026-07-06 (the interface decision): all conversation moments are Cowork captures of THIS conversation, machine moments are live terminal recordings, B04 is Bear's own photo (pending upload) — paid spend is now narration + ONE ai still (B18, which must stay generated: it demonstrates the STAND-IN X). B22 narration rewritten to match; audio regen --only B22. No bio kicker (relevance gate: no load-bearing person).

beats.narration_text: Every film in this style is assembled the same way: mixed sources, one treatment, one flat plane. This film teaches that way — by building itself.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Every film in this style is assembled the same way: mixed sources, one treatment, one flat plane. This film teaches that way — by building itself.

shot.note: cold open — Cowork composer screenshot of THE ORIGIN PROMPT of this conversation, tight crop on the message, light mode, no chrome; set shot.focus on the words 'Make it meta'

beats.new_visual_element: [SCREEN] the origin prompt in the Cowork composer — the film's first cause, on newsprint

beats.narration_text: This is a Vox-style explainer about making Vox-style explainers. The example is the video you're watching. The tool is called Unreal Reels.
beats.tts_normalized_text: This is a Vox-style explainer about making Vox-style explainers. The example is the video you're watching. The tool is called Unreal Reels.

beats.narration_text: The style is not a look — it's a laundering function. Archival photos, AI stills, film scans: each passes through one treatment and lands on one plane.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The style is not a look — it's a laundering function. Archival photos, A.I. stills, film scans: each passes through one treatment and lands on one plane.

viz.note: three visibly mismatched plates slide in, pass a treatment bar, emerge matched on the cream plane; treatment numbers are claims — verify against SKILL.md tokens

beats.narration_text: The treatment, by the numbers: desaturate about eighty percent, push the contrast, seat everything on a real newspaper scan. Sources never match. The treatment does.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The treatment, by the numbers: desaturate about eighty percent, push the contrast, seat everything on a real newspaper scan. Sources never match. The treatment does.

shot.note: BEAR'S OWN saturated photograph (pending upload), shown POST-treatment; parameter chips on the plane label −80% sat / ×1.15 contrast / newsprint ground. Needs strong color to drain — the before/after tension is the beat.

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO — Bear's own] a saturated photograph, treated to the house look; chips carry the numbers

beats.narration_text: Then the restraint that keeps it alive: two accent colors, serif labels with hairline underlines, one editor's pen per graphic. Decoration that isn't information gets deleted.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Then the restraint that keeps it alive: two accent colors, serif labels with hairline underlines, one editor's pen per graphic. Decoration that isn't information gets deleted.

tokens: one editor's pen: ring OR highlighter
data.deleted: a decorative gradient flourish appears and is struck with the ink X

viz.note: the StrikeX lands on the flourish as 'gets deleted' is spoken

beats.narration_text: Underneath every film sits one file: a beat sheet. This is this film's — and the highlighted entry is the beat you are watching right now.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Underneath every film sits one file: a beat sheet. This is this film's — and the highlighted entry is the beat you are watching right now.

shot.note: screen-grab of THIS file, cropped so the B06 entry is readable; highlighter bar via annotations.json (needs_review until the grab exists). HARVEST: after audio lock so actual_duration_s shows real numbers.

beats.narration_text: Law one: audio is the master clock. The narration is generated first, then measured. This sentence lasts exactly as long as it lasts. The picture obeys.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Law one: audio is the master clock. The narration is generated first, then measured. This sentence lasts exactly as long as it lasts. The picture obeys.

data.source: THIS sheet's measured actual_duration_s values, drawn as bars; B07's own bar fills in real time as the beat plays

viz.note: RECURSIVE DATA — bars must be regenerated from the sheet AFTER audio lock; render this scene last among graphics

beats.narration_text: Law two: every beat declares its form — still, footage, document, graphic, card — and its source: archive, AI, or your own. Form locks early. Source swaps late.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Law two: every beat declares its form — still, footage, document, graphic, card — and its source: archive, A.I., or your own. Form locks early. Source swaps late.

data.callout: this film's own beats plotted as squares in their cells

viz.note: counts from THIS sheet: STILL 4 · FOOTAGE 4 · DOCUMENT 4 · GRAPHIC 7 · CARD 4 · COMPOSITE 3 (COMPOSITE noted as STILL+plane in a footnote chip) — verify at factcheck

beats.narration_text: Because they're separate, media is swappable. Every beat is a slot. Drop a better file in and rebuild — only that slot recompiles. Watch: one slot, changing.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Because they're separate, media is swappable. Every beat is a slot. Drop a better file in and rebuild — only that slot recompiles. Watch: one slot, changing.

shot.note: screen capture: vox_compile.py rebuild on THIS reel — one changed slot recompiles, the rest report 'unchanged'. HARVEST: during any fill-phase rebuild. Key action early — the tail trims.

beats.narration_text: The pipeline is seven phases, and every gate belongs to you. Here is this film, moving through all seven.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The pipeline is seven phases, and every gate belongs to you. Here is this film, moving through all seven.

card.title: SEVEN PHASES, FIVE GATES
card.sub: plan · factcheck · audio · run · stills · video · assemble

beats.narration_text: Phase one: the plan. The script becomes beats of twenty-eight words or fewer, each with a shot type, a source, and a prompt. This paragraph is beat eleven.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Phase one: the plan. The script becomes beats of twenty-eight words or fewer, each with a shot type, a source, and a prompt. This paragraph is beat eleven.

shot.note: screen-grab of THIS script.md scrolled to the B11 entry; highlighter bar on the paragraph as 'beat eleven' is spoken. HARVEST: any time after plan approval.

beats.narration_text: Phase two: the fact check. Every claim gets a verdict and a source before anything renders. The machine refuses to build a frame without this file.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Phase two: the fact check. Every claim gets a verdict and a source before anything renders. The machine refuses to build a frame without this file.

shot.note: screen-grab of THIS reel's FACTCHECK.md; terracotta ring on the verdict column. HARVEST: after Gate F is written. Gate F enforcement claim verified against vox_run.sh.

beats.narration_text: Phase three: audio lock. A cloned voice reads every beat; a script measures each duration to the millisecond. From here on, the timing never changes.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Phase three: audio lock. A cloned voice reads every beat; a script measures each duration to the millisecond. From here on, the timing never changes.

shot.note: screen capture: generate_audio.py running on THIS reel, mp3/ filling, durations printing. HARVEST: during the actual audio lock — record the real run.

beats.narration_text: Phase four: the machine pass — one command, free, local. It audits the scenes, renders the graphics to the measured durations, and compiles a watchable cut.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Phase four: the machine pass — one command, free, local. It audits the scenes, renders the graphics to the measured durations, and compiles a watchable cut.

shot.note: screen capture: vox_run.sh on THIS reel — Gate A, Manim renders, compile. HARVEST: during the first real machine pass.

beats.narration_text: Slots you haven't filled render as slates, like this one. That's the trick: the first cut is always watchable, so you judge pacing before spending anything on pictures.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Slots you haven't filled render as slates, like this one. That's the trick: the first cut is always watchable, so you judge pacing before spending anything on pictures.

shot.note: RECURSIVE: a frame of THIS beat's own slate, grabbed from the pass-1 cut (ffmpeg frame extract) and dropped back in as the media. B15 shows B15. HARVEST: after the first vox_run pass.

beats.narration_text: Phase five: fill the slots. The shot list is a typed work order — a ready-to-run prompt for every generated image, archive links for every real one.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Phase five: fill the slots. The shot list is a typed work order — a ready-to-run prompt for every generated image, archive links for every real one.

shot.note: screen-grab of THIS SHOTLIST.md at the B01 prompt block; slow zoom-to-passage. HARVEST: after plan approval.

beats.narration_text: Raw finds land in a pantry, restored and beat-prefixed. One command strips audio, crops documents, renames everything into its slot. Rebuild — only the changed slots recompile.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Raw finds land in a pantry, restored and beat-prefixed. One command strips audio, crops documents, renames everything into its slot. Rebuild — only the changed slots recompile.

shot.note: screen capture: vox_pantry.py intake on THIS reel — renames scrolling, sidecar stubs written. HARVEST: during the real pantry intake for this film's own media.

beats.narration_text: Provenance rides along. Real people and real events require real archives, with a credit sidecar. A generated stand-in wears an ink X until the real thing arrives.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Provenance rides along. Real people and real events require real archives, with a credit sidecar. A generated stand-in wears an ink X until the real thing arrives.

shot.note: deliberately GENERIC generated portrait (no real person) wearing the hand-drawn StrikeX; sidecar chip on the plane reads 'B18.source.txt — ai / disclosed'. The X is honest here: this IS a generated stand-in.

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] anonymous 1920s-newsroom portrait, generic face, wearing the ink X

beats.narration_text: Phase six: annotation. Rings, labels, and highlights live on a separate plane, keyed to word timestamps — this ring lands exactly... now. The media below is never touched.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Phase six: annotation. Rings, labels, and highlights live on a separate plane, keyed to word timestamps — this ring lands exactly... now. The media below is never touched.

viz.note: exploded 3-layer diagram; the demo ring on the top layer is plane-side (annotations.json), keyed to the word 'now' — the film's own machinery performing its own explanation

beats.narration_text: The vertical Short is a derivative, never a re-edit. Landscape composes side by side; portrait stacks top and bottom. Same beats, re-banded. Nothing renders twice.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The vertical Short is a derivative, never a re-edit. Landscape composes side by side; portrait stacks top and bottom. Same beats, re-banded. Nothing renders twice.

data.left: 16:9 frame, two elements side by side

viz.note: the two elements physically slide from side-by-side into a vertical stack — transform, don't cut

beats.narration_text: Cuts before the last wear a burn-in — timecode, beat number, slot status — so notes land on exact frames. The clean master is the same build, unmarked.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Cuts before the last wear a burn-in — timecode, beat number, slot status — so notes land on exact frames. The clean master is the same build, unmarked.

shot.note: RECURSIVE: a frame from THIS reel's --review cut showing the burn-in (TC + beat id + slot status). HARVEST: after the first review compile. The clean master shows the review cut's frame — the one place the burn-in is allowed in the final film, because it IS the subject.

beats.narration_text: The cost: the engine is free and local. You pay only for voice and pictures. This film bought a narration and one still. Everything else was free.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The cost: the engine is free and local. You pay only for voice and pictures. This film bought a narration and one still. Everything else was free.

data.free_squares: one square per free unit: 26 beats compiled, 7 graphics rendered, all screen-grabs, the Short
data.mark: square, one unit per mark, count-up reveal in the beat's audio window

viz.note: COUNTS ARE CLAIMS — paid = 1 narration + 1 still (B18, the stand-in demo). Revised 2026-07-06: B01/B25 went interface-own, B04 is Bear's own photo. Audio regenerated --only B22 after the narration change.

beats.narration_text: The loop closes. The beat sheet you saw, the shot list, the fact check — every document in this film is its own. It rendered itself into evidence.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The loop closes. The beat sheet you saw, the shot list, the fact check — every document in this film is its own. It rendered itself into evidence.

shot.note: the three treated document scans (beat_sheet, SHOTLIST, FACTCHECK) collaged on the plane, serif labels with hairline underlines naming each. HARVEST: last — after all three documents are final.

beats.narration_text: The pipeline is called Unreal Reels. It's open source; the engine runs free on your machine. Clone the repo — the tutorial that planned this film ships inside.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The pipeline is called Unreal Reels. It's open source; the engine runs free on your machine. Clone the repo — the tutorial that planned this film ships inside.

beats.narration_text: You've just watched a film teach its own making. Yours is simpler: one story, a dozen beats, one afternoon. The machine does the assembly. The gates are yours.
beats.tts_normalized_text: You've just watched a film teach its own making. Yours is simpler: one story, a dozen beats, one afternoon. The machine does the assembly. The gates are yours.

shot.note: bookend of B01 — Cowork screen recording of a REAL AskUserQuestion gate being clicked (the shorts drop-list gate, raised live for the recording); the click lands near the spoken line 'the gates are yours'. Light mode, cropped to the window.

beats.new_visual_element: [SCREEN] the gate widget, clicked — the film's thesis performed

beats.narration_text: Next: the same engine with a different clock — a song instead of a voice — turning music into a beat-synced video.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Next: the same engine with a different clock — a song instead of a voice — turning music into a beat-synced video.
